documentation:
Playbook content support the following string format values:
- {ansible_playbooks_path}: replace with the ansible-playbook resource root folder path
- {modifiers}: Add modifier flags: DRY_RUN / VERBOSE / SILENT

Try to get the value from a nested dictionary by a sequence of keys.

```
param keys

A dot-separated string of keys.

return

The value if it exists, None otherwise.
```

classes.name: provisioner_shared.components.runtime.config.domain.config_test.ProvisionerConfigTestShould

poetry run coverage run -m pytest provisioner/config/domain/config_test.py

functions.name: provisioner_shared.components.runtime.cli.typer_callbacks.MutuallyExclusiveGroup
documentation:
Enforce mutual exclusivity on two CLI options.
Limit to x2 options, we want to keep CLI usage simple
If there are more than x2 mutual exclusive options, maybe a sub-command should be used
Usage:
option_1: str = typer.Option(
    None,
    help="Option 1 description",
    callback=exclusivity_callback,
),
option_2: str = typer.Option(
    None,
    help="Option 2 description",
    callback=exclusivity_callback,
),

class_variables.name: provisioner_shared.components.runtime.utils.properties.Properties.config_parser
documentation:
Config parser requires that the properties file will have a fake head section i.e. [Something]
It is becuase this library used to read *.ini files.
We're faking it so the user won't have to add such redunadant header

methods.name: provisioner_shared.components.runtime.utils.yaml_util.YamlUtil._read_string
documentation: def _write_to_file(self, file_path: str, yaml_text: str) -> bool:
logger.error(f"Failed to write to YAML file. ex: {str(ex)}")

List of colors can be found on the following link:

methods.name: provisioner_shared.components.runtime.utils.paths.Paths._get_path_from_exec_module_root
documentation:
Return the root folder path of the current executing project, requires a __file__ parameter
so the starting CWD will be the actual Python file within the virtual env or pip-pkg
and not from this IO utility file

methods.name: provisioner_shared.components.runtime.utils.progress_indicator.ProgressIndicator.Status._inc_based_status_indicator
documentation:
Display status indicator based on expected time increments.
Complete early if future completes.
Wait for future if it doesn't complete in expected_time.
